name: security
description: The run-time security surface: binding contributions to the docker run argv (network egress lock, pinned remote gateway, ephemeral per-role identity agent), the credential-delegation interface (broker handles, user-supplied resolver, degraded raw-token mode), and the isolation runtime knob. Every control is enforced from outside the container or is immutable relative to it.

requirements.title: Network binding egress lock
requirements.description: A workflow-level network binding attaches each step container to a named internal docker network (no route out) and injects proxy environment (HTTPS_PROXY/HTTP_PROXY pointing at the user's allow-listing egress proxy, NO_PROXY for on-net companion services). The proxy and its allow-list are user-owned companion services faber treats as opaque. Alternative mode: baked nftables rules loaded by a root entrypoint (--cap-add NET_ADMIN) that drops to the non-root agent user.

requirements.title: Remote binding pinned gateway
requirements.description: A remote binding gives the box its only reachable git remote: a gateway URL (e.g. ssh://git@<host>/<path>/<repo>.git) with SSH host-key policy — pinned key (StrictHostKeyChecking=yes, fail closed), TOFU (accept-new, sandbox only, explicit opt-in), or abort when neither is configured. The gateway itself (push validation, credential holding, PR mediation) is a user companion service; faber only sees a git URL.

requirements.title: Identity binding role keys
requirements.description: Each template declares an identity; per step, faber spawns an ephemeral ssh-agent holding exactly one key for that identity, forwards only the socket into the container (-v sock:/ssh-agent, SSH_AUTH_SOCK), and tears the agent down when the step ends. One key per container gives role isolation by construction; the private key never enters the box. Key material acquisition (file key, hardware-resident key) is resolver-supplied; the in-box prelude derives the git signing key from the forwarded agent.

requirements.title: Credential broker handles
requirements.description: API credentials are delegated as handles to out-of-container brokers, per-tool: an ssh-agent socket for keys, a base-URL rewrite to an auth-injecting local proxy for HTTP tokens, or a credential-helper endpoint. The get_token(service) resolver is an opaque user command faber invokes host-side. Degraded mode (explicit opt-in): resolve the raw token host-side (in RAM) and deliver it into a container tmpfs over the container's stdin (never a host file, never env), materialized to a 0600 file at /run/secrets/<service> that dies with the container.

requirements.title: Isolation runtime knob
requirements.description: An optional per-template or workflow-level runtime setting maps to docker run --runtime (e.g. runsc/gVisor on Linux) for hardened container isolation. Default is the platform runtime; the knob changes only the argv, never the image.

requirements.title: Deferred: secret expiry mid-run
requirements.description: Backlog (design edge case 7): behavior when a delegated credential expires mid-run (OAuth token lapse, forwarded agent socket invalidation) — detection, refresh policy, and whether a step restarts or resumes. First pass treats expiry as ordinary step failure.

requirements.title: Deferred: egress allow-list ownership
requirements.description: Backlog (design edge case 12): who declares egress allow-list contents per endpoint class (Anthropic vs OpenAI vs local) and where they live — today the allow-list is entirely inside the user's proxy config; the open question is whether templates should declare endpoint needs that faber cross-checks against the proxy.

requirements.title: Deferred: gateless trust boundary
requirements.description: Backlog (design edge case 13): workflows with no push target (research runs) have nothing for a gateway to validate — define the trust boundary and the durable home for terminal outputs when the remote binding is absent.

requirements.title: Role-fingerprint identity registry
requirements.description: faber keeps a global role→fingerprint registry (roles.json under faber's config home) that the user populates with `faber add-key` and inspects with `faber list-keys`. faber never creates or stores key material — only the fingerprint (SHA256:…) plus an optional label. A template's `identity: <name>` with no inline key path resolves role→fingerprint via the registry, then fingerprint→a live host key (running ssh-agent, then ~/.ssh/*.pub, then YubiKey resident keys), producing the keySource the ephemeral agent loads. An explicit identities.<name>.key (path or SHA256:…) takes precedence, so existing configs are byte-identical. Resolution failure — role absent, or no local key matches the fingerprint — is a clear prepare-time error naming the role and fingerprint. The private key never enters the box.

components.name: NetworkBinding
components.description: Produces the network portion of the run argv: --network <name>, proxy environment injection, NO_PROXY exemptions for companion services, and the alternative nftables entrypoint mode. Owns the deferred allow-list-ownership seam.

components.name: RemoteBinding
components.description: Produces the remote portion of the run contract: the gateway git URL exposed to the box and the host-key policy material (pinned key injection or TOFU flag). Owns the deferred gateless-workflow seam.

components.name: IdentityBinding
components.description: The ephemeral ssh-agent lifecycle: spawn with a private socket, load exactly one key for the template's identity via the resolver, contribute the socket mount and SSH_AUTH_SOCK env, and guarantee teardown on step exit.

components.name: RoleRegistry
components.description: The global role→fingerprint registry (roles.json under faber's config home): the add-key/list-keys operations, fingerprint and role validation, atomic writes, and the fingerprint resolution (role→fingerprint via the registry, then fingerprint→a live host key via the KeyLocator over the running agent, ~/.ssh/*.pub, and YubiKey resident keys) that feeds the IdentityBinding, with explicit-key-path precedence for backward compatibility.

components.name: CredentialBroker
components.description: The credential-delegation interface: handle shapes (socket, endpoint, credential-helper), the opaque get_token resolver invocation, and the degraded raw-token file mode that delivers the token into a container tmpfs over stdin (no host file, no shred). Owns the deferred expiry/refresh seam.

components.name: BindingSet
components.description: Composes the per-step binding contributions (network, remote, identity, credentials, runtime knob) into the ordered argv fragment and lifecycle hooks (pre-run setup, post-run teardown) that infra's ContainerRunner consumes.

impl_sections.name: Role registry store, operations, and resolution

data_flows.name: Binding assembly flow
data_flows.description: Config (workflow network + template identity + credential declarations) -> per-binding setup (agent spawn, resolver calls, host-key material) -> BindingSet composition -> ordered argv fragment + teardown hooks -> ContainerRunner.
